popularStory.headline: Life Sketch for Hugh Gaylord
popularStory.description: Hugh Gaylord was born about 1553 in Piminster, Somerset, England.  His parents were Nicholas Gaillard and Johanne Brixton.  He had an older brother and three younger brothers and two younger sisters. …

nameMeanings.name: Gaylord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern, of Norman origin): variant of Gaylard altered by folk etymology, from the Old French and Middle English personal name Gaillart, Gallard, Galliard, representing either the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard (composed of elements meaning ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + ‘hard, firm, resilient, strong’) or Old French gaillard, gaillart ‘vigorous, powerful, strong’. Alternatively, from Middle English gaillard, galiard ‘brave, strong, valiant’, also ‘lively, gay, gaily dressed’.
Altered form of Gaylard, itself an Anglicized form of French Gaillard , which was brought to England by the Huguenots.
In some cases also an Americanized form of French   Gaillard  .

variants.name: Gaillard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Philippe, Pierre, Andre, Andree, Ketly, Marcel, Mirlande, Monique.
French: nickname from gaillard ‘vigorous, strong’ (ultimately from Celtic galia ‘strength’). This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gailhard, composed of the elements gail ‘powerful, lusty, cheerful’ + hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord was born as the son of Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster and Joan Brixton Sevenoak. He had at least 8 sons and 4 daughters with Joan Alwyn. He died on 20 October 1614, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Pitminster, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord est né, fils de Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster et Joan Brixton Sevenoak. Il a eu au moins 8 fils et 4 filles avec Joan Alwyn. Il est décédé le 20 octobre 1614, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord wurde, als Sohn von Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster und Joan Brixton Sevenoak geboren. Er hatte mit Joan Alwyn mindestens 8 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 20. Oktober 1614 in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Pitminster, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord è nato il come figlio di Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster e Joan Brixton Sevenoak. Lui avuto almeno 8 figli e 4 figlie con Joan Alwyn. Morì il 20 ottobre 1614, nel Pitminster, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Pitminster, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh GaylordはNicholas Gaylord of PitminsterとJoan Brixton Sevenoakの息子として生まれた。彼はJoan Alwynとの間に少なくとも8人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1614年10月20日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトピトミンスターで死去し，イギリスソメルセトピトミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord(은)는 Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster(와)과 Joan Brixton Sevenoak의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Joan Alwyn(와)과 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1614년 10월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에서 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 피트민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord nasceu filho de Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster e Joan Brixton Sevenoak. Ele teve pelo menos 8 filhos e 4 filhas com Joan Alwyn. Ele faleceu em 20 de outubro de 1614, em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord родился. Его родителей звали Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster и Joan Brixton Sevenoak. У него было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 4 дочери от Joan Alwyn. Он умер 20 октября 1614 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Hugh Gaylord nació como hijo de Nicholas Gaylord of Pitminster y Joan Brixton Sevenoak. Tuvo por lo menos 8 hijos y 4 hijas con Joan Alwyn. Murió el 20 de octubre de 1614, en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Pitminster, Somerset, Inglaterra.
